title: [Bug]: HTTP sink NGINX FIPS test endpoint should recreate tmpfs runtime directories
summary: The new HTTP sink NGINX FIPS test endpoint container can fail to start when the e2e runner uses a read-only root filesystem and tmpfs-mounted runtime paths.
observed: The live HTTP sink container e2e run built and started the image, but NGINX exited before serving the health endpoint because /tmp/nginx/client_body was missing after /tmp was mounted as tmpfs at runtime.
expected: The container entrypoint should recreate all tmpfs-backed runtime directories before starting NGINX so the endpoint remains compatible with the hardened read-only run flags used by the local e2e runner.
reproduction: Run python scripts/run-http-sink-nginx-e2e.py --timeout-seconds 30 on the issue #344 branch before the entrypoint fix.
failing_test: tests/unit/test_http_nginx_test_container.py should prove the entrypoint recreates /tmp/nginx/client_body, /tmp/nginx/proxy, and /var/lib/nats-sinks-http before NGINX starts.
impact: The defect blocks the new local HTTP sink container e2e test. It does not affect production JetStream ACK behavior, existing sinks, or production HTTP sink delivery semantics.
delivery_semantics: The failure happens before the local test endpoint accepts traffic. No JetStream message is ACKed by this container, and the production HTTP sink still reports failure when endpoint success is not confirmed.
security: The fix must preserve read-only-root compatibility, loopback-only exposure, non-root execution, dropped capabilities, no-new-privileges, fake payloads only, and sanitized evidence.
acceptance: The entrypoint creates the tmpfs-backed NGINX client-body and proxy temp directories before starting NGINX.
The entrypoint creates the capture directory before starting the loopback capture helper.
A focused regression test checks the entrypoint runtime directory creation commands.
python scripts/run-http-sink-nginx-e2e.py passes against the local Docker backend.
Sanitized failing and passing test evidence is posted to the GitHub bug report.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_http_nginx_test_container.py -q, scripts/check-sinks.sh, scripts/check.sh, and python scripts/run-http-sink-nginx-e2e.py --timeout-seconds 120.
documentation: Mention the runtime-directory hardening in the HTTP sink NGINX FIPS test endpoint documentation if the public test contract changes.
closeout: Mark completed after the focused regression test and live Docker e2e runner pass, then merge the fix back into the issue #344 feature branch.
